task:
Compare Hyprland v0.55.4 specialWorkspace slidevert animation implementation/timing with Qt Quick client-side animation. Inspect exact source paths for render offset updates and compositor scheduling. Explain why compositor-native motion may look smoother and which aspects can/cannot be replicated in Quickshell. Return GitHub permalinks and practical implications; do not edit files.

---
**Output:**
Write your findings to exactly this path: /home/ian/.config/nixos/.pi-subagents/artifacts/outputs/1add01a4-bb1b-4fbe-8a76-cbd5f5648b56/hyprland-animation-research.md
This path is authoritative for this run.
Ignore any other output filename or output path mentioned elsewhere, including output destinations in the base agent prompt, system prompt, or task instructions.

criteria.must: Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable
Return concrete findings with file paths and severity when applicable

criteriaSatisfied.evidence: Concrete severity-tagged findings identify src/managers/animation/DesktopAnimationManager.cpp, src/managers/animation/AnimationManager.cpp, src/desktop/Workspace.cpp, src/render/Renderer.cpp, and src/helpers/MonitorFrameScheduler.cpp with immutable GitHub permalinks; residual risks are explicitly listed.

commandsRun.command: read-only source inspection of Hyprland v0.55.4 tag and authoritative Qt/Quickshell documentation
commandsRun.summary: Resolved v0.55.4 to immutable commit a0136d8c04687bb36eb8a28eb9d1ff92aea99704 and inspected animation, renderer, and scheduler paths.

validationOutput: Verified v0.55.4 tag SHA via GitHub API.
Verified slidevert endpoints, special-workspace alpha, animated-variable update path, damage/frame request, render-time offset consumption, and monitor frame scheduling in tagged source.
Verified Qt threaded/basic animation-driver behavior and multi-window fallback in official Qt documentation.
residualRisks: User-specific animation leaf speed and Bézier were not provided.
No empirical frame-time benchmark was run on the target GPU/monitor topology.
Exact pinned hyprutils getCurveStep implementation was not line-inspected.
childReport.diffSummary: Research-only output; no repository files were edited.
reviewFindings: high capability boundary: Quickshell cannot atomically transform arbitrary workspace clients or control Hyprland output scheduling.
medium practical mismatch: client rendering/Wayland commit adds a scheduling boundary and Qt may use timer-driven animation with multiple QQuickWindows.
no blockers in the inspected Hyprland implementation; limitations are architectural rather than a source defect.
childReport.manualNotes: The requested research artifact was written to the runtime-authoritative output path; the NixOS repository itself was not modified.
